# Transcrição: 02-Setting Up Your Ads Library

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So one of the first parts that we do when we build accounts is to create what I call the ads library. And what this allows us to do is create a storage place as a filing system for our various ads. So the way I would do that is create a dummy campaign, make sure it's switched off, you know, use a small budget, just make sure it's switched off and clearly labeled, do not launch. So we click into that, we create ad sets, and I'm gonna switch these off because they should always be switched off. You create your ad sets and within the ad sets, and you know, it could just be any kind of dummy targeting just with time being, but it's a name that's important. So here we've named these four ad sets based on the different parts of our funnel. So we've got our cold ads, we've got warm, hot and existing customer ads. And then we've also got cold ads for different angles, just to make it easier for us to reference. So going back to the 5W avatar, for example, these are core angles that we're playing off. So for example, if I go into my cold ads, the general folder, there's a couple of ads in there, and these are here for a couple of reasons. Number one, it makes it easier to reference all the ads you've got. So, you know, I used to have all my ads listed in the spreadsheet, it was hard to maintain, and kind of working between the spreadsheet and ads manager was a bit difficult. So what I did was to create that same filing system here, and it just makes it so much easier because everyone knows where to go, it's in the ads manager, it's filed based on the part of the funnel, an angle where relevant as well. You can have multiple ad sets here, I mean, this is a closed campaign, it's fine. And then within there, we have our ads. And what we do is when we drop the ads in, we get the post ID. So once you've created the ad here, you get the post ID for ads that can have post ID, and there's a separate training on that. You get the post ID and save it into the name of the ad. So this is a little hack. So this is the ad that we've got here, the ad name, and at the end, we put the post ID. What this enables you to do is that when you copy this ad, and you go and paste it in the campaign that needs it, what Facebook does is create a new ad. But when you take that ad into that new campaign, you can take the post ID and quickly copy it over. So let me just show you what I mean by that. So let's say for example, I wanna copy this ad, and I want to use it in one of my test campaigns. So let's go to phase two. So let's say we're in phase two of our testing, and we want to drop this ad into one of these campaigns. What I can do is if I just drop it here,

## [03:02 - 06:04]

so I'll paste it into here, and we'll go into the ad level, and I'll show you how that looks. Just waiting for Facebook to update. So now Facebook has created that ad. It will create a copy of the ad, but we won't use the same ad by ID. So what we can do here, just to save a bit of time, is the ad ID's already been copied over into this part of the ad here. So I'm just gonna copy that post ID, because it's from the original post. And then let's just check. Oh, in fact, it's already copied over. So that's even easier. In some cases, it doesn't. So I would go into here, use existing post, and then copy that ad ID over. It's done it for me already. And that enables me to share the social proof for the ad. So for example, when I'm in phase two testing, if I find that this ad works, then I will also copy it to my page post engagement campaign for social proof. And then I would copy it over to my main campaigns. And by sharing the post ID, you do share the social proof, and you also share part of the history of the ad as well. That's just something to bear in mind when you are copying ads over. Make sure where possible you're sharing the post ID, doesn't work for all ads, but for many of them, it should. So some ads, it doesn't work on our carousel, instant experience, and a few others. So going back to the ads library, the key thing to take away from here is create a filing system for your different ads based on where they are in the funnel. So this isn't a filing system for your audiences. So the ad targeting in these different ads sets is not important, but it's designed specifically to store ads so that we have an easy, referenceable area where we can go and look at all the ads in play, and we can see what we've already published. For example, on some ad accounts, you'll have multiple media buyers. It makes it easier for everyone to work together on what's in process, what's happening, et cetera. And the other thing that you can do as well is two options. One is as you go through the testing phase, if you find ads do not work, then you can edit the ad name and make it clear that it was tested and it did not work so that if you are working with multiple media buyers, then that other person knows that this ad was not a good ad, don't use it. But on the flip side, you could have an ad that works in some audiences and not in others. So you don't wanna overly put too much information here. You'll be able to see that from your creative campaign view where you can actually look at the ads by name and look at the performance across different ad sets. But the information here is useful for you on an ongoing basis to know which ads are working.

## [06:04 - 06:41]

The other option is if you do test ads and they don't work, you can just delete them from here and just make sure that no one else comes into these folders and uses the ad as well. So it's a useful way for me to reference the ads. I think you'll find it's easier too, certainly easier than using spreadsheets and copying ad names across and things like that and kind of knowing what your story is, what kind of ads you've got, what kind of video ads and angles and things like that. So I would definitely recommend creating something similar for your own purposes, just to have as a reference point for you and your team if you have one helping as well.
